description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a military green fuselage, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires. The upper and lower wings both display roundel insignias, indicating military or historical use, and the tail is marked with a tricolor rudder and serial numbers. The nose houses a wooden propeller, and the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a single seat visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The aircraft's surface is mostly matte, with visible panel lines and rivets, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter planes. The structure appears to be primarily metal with some wooden and fabric components, typical of classic biplane construction.
description: A vintage military biplane with green fuselage, dual stacked wings, roundel insignias, and an open cockpit behind a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Side view showing the green fuselage, open cockpit, roundel insignias on the wings, and the wooden propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual stacked wings, propeller, and the symmetrical placement of roundel insignias on both wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail markings, open cockpit, and the fixed landing gear with two main wheels.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, tricolor rudder, and the alignment of the biplane's wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; tricolor rudder present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing; appears to be single-seat with no canopy.
